Buyer's question

AI Consultant San Francisco / Bay Area Remote-first AI consulting for Bay Area B2B.

Treetop works with San Francisco and Bay Area B2B companies remotely. We're not the right firm for Bay Area B2B AI consulting in every case — here's an honest take on when we're the right fit and when you should look elsewhere.

Short answer

Treetop works with Bay Area B2B companies remote-first. We're particularly relevant for B2B SaaS at \$5M-\$30M ARR rolling out Claude or ChatGPT Team. We're not the right firm for very large enterprises or pure AI engineering work — those need different shops.

By Bill Colbert · Founder, Treetop Growth Strategy
Published May 2026 · More from the library

When Treetop is the right fit for Bay Area B2B

  • B2B SaaS at \$5M-\$30M ARR with messy or absent AI rollout.
  • Need someone who has shipped real workflows, not just produced strategy decks.
  • Want AI rollout that compounds with marketing/sales execution, not just an isolated AI initiative.
  • Remote-first works for the engagement (we'll travel for kickoffs and quarterly check-ins, not weekly).

When you should look elsewhere

  • Enterprise (1,000+ people). Different operating model required; pick a firm specialized in enterprise AI rollout.
  • Pure AI engineering or model development. We do not build foundation models or custom LLMs. Pick an AI engineering shop.
  • Need someone on-site full-time. Remote-first model means we cannot be in your office daily.
  • Pre-product-market-fit startups. AI rollout is not the priority; fix the product.

How we work with Bay Area clients

  • AI Audit (\$1,500) as a low-commitment first engagement — produces a written roadmap.
  • Fixed-fee implementation (\$5K-\$50K) for 1-3 production workflows.
  • Quarterly on-site visits to SF for major milestones.
  • Weekly remote cadence in between.

Bay Area context that shapes our work

  • Higher baseline AI fluency in your team than at most clients — adoption curve is faster.
  • Existing tool stacks are often more sprawling — audit usually surfaces overlap and waste.
  • Buyer expectations on the company's AI usage are higher — having a credible AI story matters for sales.
  • Talent market for AI ops roles is competitive — hiring takes longer than other markets.

FAQ

Do you have an SF office?

No. Remote-first. On-site visits to SF for client engagements.

Have you worked with Bay Area B2B SaaS?

Yes — substantial portion of our practice.

Do you offer on-site presence beyond quarterly?

Negotiable per engagement. Some clients have engaged us for monthly visits; not standard.

What's your AI Audit deliverable?

Written, prioritized roadmap covering current state, recommended workflows, platform recommendation, 90-day plan, budget estimate. 1-3 weeks.

Do you compete with larger consultancies on Bay Area engagements?

Sometimes. We're typically faster and more execution-focused; larger firms typically have more analytical depth and more layers.
